# Feature: Scan sufficiency gate

<!-- auto-generated by /implement on 2026-07-17 — do not edit manually unless correcting intent -->

## Intent
After analyze (source + optional playwright import + optional runtime), evaluate whether evidence is enough to build help/tours. Emit machine-readable status `sufficient | escalate | blocked` with German human reasons, written to scan output and surfaced by doctor/scan summary.

Roadmap: Autonomy batch A — PRD Playwright import first, own crawl fallback.

## Preconditions
- Core package builds; scan can produce pages/flows/facts artifacts
- No host Playwright JSON required for the gate itself

## Happy Path
- [ ] Core module documents pragmatic criteria (≥1 ordered flow OR ≥N interactive facts + pages)
- [ ] Scan writes `sufficiency.json` with status, evidence, DE reasons
- [ ] Scan warnings / doctor include German status reasons
- [ ] Unit tests cover sufficient vs escalate vs blocked
- [ ] No browo-specific hardcodes

## Edge Cases
- [ ] Empty scan (no pages/facts/flows) → blocked
- [ ] Pages + interactive facts below threshold, no flows → escalate
- [ ] ≥1 ordered flow alone → sufficient

## Regression
- [ ] Existing scan/doctor tests still pass
- [ ] FLOW_SEEDING_HINT behavior unchanged for this slice

## Assumptions
- Issue B will act on `escalate`; this slice only evaluates and reports
- Thresholds are YAGNI constants, not config knobs yet

| Step | Filename |

## Implementation Notes
- `packages/core/src/sufficiency/` — evaluateSufficiency, criteria, German reasons
- `packages/storage` — `sufficiency.json` path
- `packages/cli` scan writes report + warning; doctor prints DE reasons
- Tests: `evaluate.test.ts`, `sufficiency.integration.test.ts`
- Thresholds: ≥1 ordered flow OR (≥3 interactive facts AND ≥1 page)
